title: Waves, Active Grids, Optical Type, Jelly & Snap Sliders
description: Five live UI drops: a wave-propagation cube grid, a hover-active bento layout, a variable-font optical-sizing machine, tactile jelly buttons, and a magnetic snap-to-tick slider.

drops.title: Wave Propagation Grid
drops.description: Click anywhere to send a real height-field wave rippling across a grid of tiles, each lifting and glowing in pseudo-3D.
drops.concept: A discretized 2D wave equation (velocity + Laplacian diffusion + damping) drives a height field under a grid of canvas-drawn tiles. Each tile's screen position, scale and glow react to its local wave height, producing propagating ripples with no WebGL required.
drops.source: Codrops — 'Building an Interactive Wave Propagation Cube Grid with Three.js'

interaction.mouse: Click or drag to disturb the grid
interaction.click: Sends a ripple from that point

drops.title: Active Bento Grid
drops.description: A bento dashboard where tiles expand, glow, and reveal a hidden metrics layer on hover instead of sitting static.
drops.concept: 2026 design coverage describes an 'Active Grid' evolution of the bento trend: tiles that expand, play motion, or reveal secondary data on interaction rather than just changing color. This reimplements that with animated grid-span expansion, a radial glow layer, and a cross-fading metrics overlay.
drops.source: WriterDock — 'Bento Grids & Beyond: 7 UI Trends Dominating Web Design 2026'

interaction.mouse: Hover a tile to expand it
interaction.touch: Tap a tile to activate; tap elsewhere to close

drops.title: Optical Size Machine
drops.description: Drag the size slider and the type's weight, tracking, and stroke contrast reshape live, echoing real optical-size variable font axes.
drops.concept: Variable fonts with an 'opsz' axis render differently at small vs. large sizes — heavier, wider-set, lower-contrast strokes when small; lighter and tighter at display sizes. This demo simulates those traits with font-variation-settings plus a synthesized text-stroke and letter-spacing curve tied to the size slider, auto-animating between extremes.
drops.source: Google Fonts Knowledge & MDN — Optical Size (opsz) axis documentation

interaction.mouse: Drag the slider to change display size
interaction.click: Click the word to cycle sample text
interaction.touch: Slider and tap both work on mobile

drops.title: Jelly Buttons
drops.description: Press a button and it squishes and wobbles back into shape with real spring physics — clay and chrome, not flat color swaps.
drops.concept: 2026's 'Tactile Maximalism' trend describes buttons that look and behave like physical materials — jelly, chrome, clay — reacting to touch with squish and bounce rather than a simple color change. This demo drives a small spring simulation (stiffness/damping) on scaleX/scaleY per button, triggered on pointerdown/up.
drops.source: Medium — 'Tactile Maximalism: Why 2026's Hottest Design Trend Feels Like You Can Touch the Screen'

interaction.mouse: Press and hold, then release any button

drops.title: Smart Snap Slider
drops.description: A range slider that magnetically snaps to ticks with an elastic overshoot bounce, and range thumbs that clamp inside track bounds so they can never cross.
drops.concept: Base UI's Slider component recently added a thumbAlignment prop to keep thumbs correctly bounded within the track rather than overflowing at the extremes. This demo extends that idea with tick-snapping on release (with a small spring overshoot for feel) and neighbor-clamping so a two-handle range slider's thumbs can never swap order or overlap.

interaction.mouse: Drag either thumb; release to snap to the nearest tick
interaction.touch: Drag works with touch identically to mouse
